---
title: "EasyDE Walkthrough — 06 fGSEA"
output: html_document
---

# Step 06: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis

For each contrast × stratum, runs fGSEA using the Wald test statistic
as ranking metric. Ribosomal and mitochondrial genes are excluded before
ranking.

In paired mode, fGSEA runs on **base results only** (step 03 output).
In unpaired mode, it can run on base, RUV, or both (set `fgsea.run_on`
in config).

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
pipeline_root <- normalizePath("../..")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = pipeline_root)
setwd(pipeline_root)
config_path <- "config/config_treatments.yaml"   # EDIT THIS
```

## Source and run

```{r source-script}
source("workflow/scripts/06_run_fgsea.R")
```

```{r build-loop}
config_data <- load_config(config_path)
contrasts_df <- config_data$contrasts_df

loop_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(contrasts_df)), function(i) {
    row <- contrasts_df[i, ]
    strata <- parse_pipe_field(row$strata)
    data.frame(contrast_id = row$contrast_id, stratum = strata,
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}))
```

```{r run-loop}
for (i in seq_len(nrow(loop_table))) {
    cid <- loop_table$contrast_id[i]
    stm <- loop_table$stratum[i]
    cat(sprintf("\n=== [%d/%d] %s | %s ===\n", i, nrow(loop_table), cid, stm))

    tryCatch(
        main(config_path, cid, stm),
        error = function(e) cat(sprintf("  [ERROR] %s\n", e$message))
    )
}
```

## Check pathway counts

```{r check-pathways}
cfg <- config_data$cfg
for (i in seq_len(nrow(loop_table))) {
    cid <- loop_table$contrast_id[i]
    stm <- loop_table$stratum[i]
    fgsea_path <- file.path(cfg$outputs$results_dir, cid, stm,
                            "finals", "fgsea_base.tsv")
    if (file.exists(fgsea_path) && !is_skip_sentinel(fgsea_path)) {
        res <- read.delim(fgsea_path)
        n_sig <- sum(!is.na(res$padj) & res$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
        cat(sprintf("  %s | %s → %d significant pathways\n", cid, stm, n_sig))
    } else {
        cat(sprintf("  %s | %s → skipped\n", cid, stm))
    }
}
```
